title: A sparsh bharati e-reader system and a method thereof
abstract: The present invention discloses a Sparsh Bharati (SB) e-reader system for Indic languages and a method thereof. The proposed Sparsh Bharati e-reader system (100) is designed to empower individuals with visual impairments by providing a seamless and immersive braille reading experience. The system (100) comprises at least one user device (102), at least one Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104), a communication unit (106), and at least one server (108). The Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) comprises an input unit (202), a processing unit (204), a memory unit (206), a communication unit (208), an output unit (210), and a power supply unit (212). The Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) utilizes refreshable SB cells (500) to dynamically display SB characters, enabling users to read a wide range of digital content. Further, the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) transforms digital input into a tangible and interactive reading experience, providing a sense of touch and independence for readers.
claims: Claims ( 16 ) A system (100) with at least one Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) to enable tactile reading, comprising: the at least one Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) comprising: an input unit (202) to receive at least one text input; a processing unit (204) to process the received text input; and an output unit (210) comprising a Sparsh Bharati display (402), wherein the Sparsh Bharati display (402) comprises one or more dynamic Sparsh Bharati cells (500) to enable tactile reading of the text input The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein each Sparsh Bharati cell (500) comprises: multiple Sparsh Bharati dots (502); multiple line segments (504); and a set of pins (506) coupled to each of the dots (502) and line segments (504), to raise or lower the dots (502) and line segments (504) to form characters representing the text input. The system (100) as claimed in claim 2, comprising one or more piezoelectric crystals (508) coupled to each pin (506), wherein application of an electric field causes the piezoelectric crystals (508) to deform and move the pin (506) to raise or lower the dots (502) and line segments (504). The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein each Sparsh Bharati cell (500) comprises a grid of four raised dots (502) and twelve raised line segments (504) arranged in two columns and two rows. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, the input unit (202) being configured to access the text input by accessing the memory unit (206) within the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) or receive the text input wirelessly from a user device (102), wherein the input unit (202) comprises at least one of a keyboard (302), a touchscreen interface (304), an audio input (306), and USB/Bluetooth connectivity (308), and wherein the keyboard (302) comprises at least one of a Sparsh Bharati keyboard, and a QWERTY keyboard; an inverter module (222) configured for secure data handling and storage. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the output unit (210) comprises an audio output (404) to convert text into synthesized speech. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) is configured to communicate with at least one user device (102) via a communication network (106) to receive the text input. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) comprising a power supply unit (212) configured to provide power to the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104), wherein the power supply unit (212) comprises at least one of a rechargeable battery, a replaceable battery, and a built-in battery that can be recharged via a USB cable or a power adapter. A method (600) for enabling blind and visually impaired people to read using a system (100), the method (600) comprising: receiving at least one text input through an input unit (202); processing the received text input via a processing unit (204); and enabling tactile reading of the text input via a Sparsh Bharati display (402) in an output unit (210), wherein the Sparsh Bharati display (402) comprises one or more dynamic Sparsh Bharati cells (500). The method (600) as claimed in claim 9, comprising providing each Sparsh Bharati cell (500) with: multiple Sparsh Bharati dots (502); multiple line segments (504); and a set of pins (506) coupled to each of the dots (502) and line segments (504), for raising or lowering the dots (502) and line segments (504) to form characters representing the text input; The method (600) as claimed in claim 10, comprising providing one or more piezoelectric crystals (508) coupled to each pin (506), wherein application of an electric field causes the piezoelectric crystals (508) to deform and move the pin (506) for raising or lowering the dots (502) and line segments (504). The method (600) as claimed in claim 9, comprising providing each Sparsh Bharati cell (500) with a grid of four raised dots (502) and twelve raised line segments (504) arranged in two columns and two rows. The method (600) as claimed in claim 9, comprising configuring the input unit (202) for: accessing the text input by accessing the memory unit (206) within the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) or receive the text input wirelessly from a user device (102), wherein the input unit (202) comprises at least one of a keyboard (302), a touchscreen interface (304), an audio input (306), and USB/Bluetooth connectivity (308), and wherein the keyboard (302) comprises at least one of a Sparsh Bharati keyboard, and a QWERTY keyboard. The method (600) as claimed in claim 9, comprising providing the output unit (210) with at least one of an audio output (404) to convert text into synthesized speech. The method (600) as claimed in claim 9, comprising configuring the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) for communicating with at least one user device (102) via a communication network (106) for receiving the text input. The method (600) as claimed in claim 1, comprising providing the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104) with a power supply unit (212) configured for providing power to the Sparsh Bharati e-reader (104), wherein the power supply unit (212) comprises at least one of a rechargeable battery, a replaceable battery, and a built-in battery that can be recharged via a USB cable or a power adapter.
